title: Seoul Prosecutors Reject Arrest Warrant for HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk — Cite Insufficient Evidence
detail: On April 24, 2026, the Seoul Southern District Prosecutors' Office rejected the arrest warrant filed by the Seoul Metropolitan Police Agency against HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk, citing 'insufficient evidence to justify the necessity of detention.' The rejection comes three days after police announced the warrant request on April 21, alleging Bang defrauded early HYBE investors under the Capital Markets Act by concealing IPO plans while encouraging them to sell shares at undervalue in 2019 — allegedly netting approximately 260 billion won (~$175 million) in illicit gains. Bang has been banned from international travel since August 2025; separately, the U.S. Embassy requested a travel exemption to permit his attendance at BTS's world tour. Bang denies all wrongdoing. The warrant rejection was simultaneously confirmed by Korea Herald, UPI, Seoul Economic Daily, and Korea Times. HYBE issued no immediate comment. The development provides temporary legal breathing room for Bang as BTS opens the North American leg of the ARIRANG World Tour in Tampa the following day.

media.caption: Seoul prosecutors reject arrest warrant for HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk, citing insufficient evidence for detention

title: BTS Mania Hits Tampa — Fans Compare Scene to Beatlemania; 180,000 Expected Across Three Sold-Out Nights
detail: On April 24, 2026 — the day before the ARIRANG World Tour North America opener — FOX 13 Tampa Bay and the Tampa Bay Times published extensive coverage of the extraordinary fan mobilization descending on Tampa. FOX 13 (April 24, 6:19 AM EDT) reported fans comparing the atmosphere to Beatlemania: the three sold-out shows at Raymond James Stadium (April 25, 26, 28) mark the first-ever K-pop headline event at the venue. The Tampa Bay Times published an economic impact story titled '180,000 people will see BTS in Tampa. The impact is already huge.' — documenting a fan-organized ARMY welcome event at Tampa International Airport organized by the 'Tampa Bangtan Besties' fan group, sold-out merchandise items, and the ongoing closure of Tampa Bay Boulevard. Thousands of ARMY from across North America had arrived in the city days ahead of the opener. With each night expected to draw ~60,000–65,000 fans, the three-date Tampa run represents the largest K-pop concert event ever held in Florida.

media.caption: BTS fans flood Tampa ahead of three sold-out ARIRANG World Tour stadium shows, with scenes compared to Beatlemania
